text.title: Spring Async Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the Spring Async methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every @Async annotation MUST reference a named ThreadPoolTaskExecutor bean: `@Async("taskExecutor")`. Bare `@Async` or `@Async("")` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Bare @Async falls back to SimpleAsyncTaskExecutor (unbounded thread creation per call); under load it exhausts OS thread limits silently.

rule.statement: The @Async method MUST live in a different Spring bean than the caller. Calling `this.asyncMethod()` from inside the same class bypasses the proxy.
rule.rationale: @Async works via a Spring AOP proxy. Self-invocation skips the proxy — the method runs synchronously and silently.

rule.statement: @Async methods MUST return `CompletableFuture<T>` OR an `AsyncUncaughtExceptionHandler` MUST be registered in AsyncConfig. void-returning @Async without a handler is forbidden.

rationale.source: Spring Framework reference — Async exception handling

rule.rationale: Void @Async drops exceptions silently. Lost exceptions = lost operational visibility on email/webhook failures.

rule.statement: @Async and @Transactional MUST NOT be combined on the same method. Either the caller opens no tx, or the async method opens its own.
rule.rationale: @Transactional binds a JDBC connection to the calling thread via ThreadLocal. The async thread is a different thread — no tx propagation.

rule.statement: Every ThreadPoolTaskExecutor MUST install a TaskDecorator that copies MDC context map, SecurityContextHolder, and OTel current span to the async thread.
rule.rationale: MDC (trace IDs), SecurityContext, OTel span are ThreadLocal. Without propagation, async logs are untraced and SecurityContext is null.

rule.statement: Every ThreadPoolTaskExecutor MUST set `setWaitForTasksToCompleteOnShutdown(true)` and a bounded `setAwaitTerminationSeconds`.
rule.rationale: Without shutdown drain, in-flight tasks are dropped on deploy; emails/webhook calls silently lost.
